technology: Obsidian-compatible vault + SQLite + filesystem MCP (memory architecture)
source: Community consensus research (Mem0, OpenViking, memweave, Karpathy LLM Wiki) + Obsidian headless research
summary: Memory architecture: Obsidian-compatible markdown vault + SQLite (memweave) + tiered loading + filesystem MCP server

community_evidence.production_deployments: Mem0 ($6.27B market), memweave (hybrid search), Google Memory Agent (2-table pattern), LinkedIn CMA
community_evidence.practitioner_sentiment: Community converged on graph+vector hybrid with tiered loading. Flat markdown outdated. Memory is the #1 failure cause.

evaluation.cost_of_switching: memweave: pip install. MCPVault: npm install. Structure wiki pages with Summary/Overview/Details headers.
evaluation.cost_of_staying: Flat wiki loads everything every time (no tiered loading), no search index, no structured queries, no concurrent access safety

rationale: Memory is the moat — community consensus. Three components: (1) Obsidian-compatible markdown vault with [[wikilinks]] as human-readable source of truth. Obsidian app does NOT work headless — use filesystem MCP server (MCPVault or obsidian-mcp) on VPS, Obsidian on desktop for John. (2) SQLite via memweave for derived search index (FTS5 + vector, hybrid scoring, rebuildable from markdown). (3) Tiered loading (L0 summary/L1 overview/L2 details per OpenViking pattern) — 80-90% of queries resolve at L1, 83% token reduction. SOUL.md pattern for identity persistence.

test_results.notes: memweave, MCPVault, obsidian-mcp all researched with install commands. Schema designed for decisions, research findings, technology radar. Wiki pages already have L0/L1 headers but need restructuring to Summary/Overview/Details format.

rollback.instructions: Revert to OpenClaw native memory (MEMORY.md + daily files + Tier 3 search)
